chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail Application No.S-613 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on MA No.3689/2025 2. For orders on office objection at flag `A` 3. For orders on MA No.3690/2025 4. For hearing of bail application 21.07.2025 Mr. Asif Ali Samore, Advocate for the Applicant/Accused.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-- 1. Granted. 2. One week’s time granted for compliance of office objections. 3. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 4. Through instant bail application, applicant/accused Muhammad Qabool S/O Abdul Majeed, by caste Rajper, seeks pre-arrest bail in FIR No.63/2025, registered at Police Station Setharja, District Khairpur, u/s 354-A, 337A(i) & 34 PPC. Earlier his pre-arrest bail plea was declined by learned Additional Sessions Judge-IV, Khairpur vide order dated 16.7.2025. Learned counsel for the applicant contended that the applicant is innocent, who has been falsely involved in this case with malafide intention and ulterior motive due to previous enmity. He also contended that there is no independent witness of the incident. He further contended that there is unexplained delay of about one month and three days in lodging of FIR. He further contended that the applicant apprehend his imminent arrest at the hands of police as the concerned police at the behest of complainant is bent upon to arrest, humiliate and disgrace him, therefore, he prays for grant of pre- arrest bail. Without touching the merits of the case, applicant is admitted to interim pre-arrest bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) and P.R bonds in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court.

He further contended that the applicant apprehend his imminent arrest at the hands of police as the concerned police at the behest of complainant is bent upon to arrest, humiliate and disgrace him, therefore, he prays for grant of pre- arrest bail. Without touching the merits of the case, applicant is admitted to interim pre-arrest bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) and P.R bonds in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Issue notice to complainant as well as Addl.PG. Applicant is directed to join investigation/ trial after furnishing requisite surety and submit such proof on the next date of hearing. To come up on 11.8.2025 for confirmation or otherwise. JUDGE S. Nawaz(St)’
